chapter: Chapter: Civilising the Native, Educating the Nation

questions.question: What was the indigenous system of education in India?
questions.answer: It was the traditional system of education conducted in pathshalas, gurukuls, and madrassas before British rule.

questions.question: Who were the Orientalists in the education debate?
questions.answer: They were scholars who supported Indian languages and classical texts in education.

questions.question: Who were the Anglicists?
questions.answer: They were people who supported English education and Western knowledge.

questions.question: What was the main objective of Macaulay’s Minute of 1835?
questions.answer: To promote English education and Western knowledge in India.

questions.question: Why did the British prefer English education?
questions.answer: They wanted to create educated Indians who could assist in administration.

questions.question: What important recommendation was made in Wood’s Despatch?
questions.answer: The establishment of universities in India.

questions.question: Why is Wood’s Despatch considered important in Indian education?
questions.answer: Because it laid the foundation for the modern education system in India.

questions.question: Which universities were established in India in 1857?
questions.answer: Calcutta, Bombay, and Madras.

questions.question: Who supported modern scientific education in India?
questions.answer: Raja Rammohan Roy.

questions.question: What type of class emerged due to English education?
questions.answer: An educated middle class.

questions.question: What happened to traditional schools during British rule?
questions.answer: They gradually declined in importance.

questions.question: What subjects were introduced through Western education?
questions.answer: Science, mathematics, and English literature.

questions.question: What political ideas spread through modern education?
questions.answer: Ideas of democracy, equality, and liberty.

questions.question: How did Western education influence Indian society?
questions.answer: It spread new ideas and created an educated middle class.

questions.question: What role did education play in social reform movements?
questions.answer: It helped spread awareness about social problems.

questions.question: How did modern education contribute to the freedom movement?
questions.answer: It created political awareness among Indians.

questions.question: Why did some Indians criticize colonial education?
questions.answer: Because it ignored traditional Indian knowledge.

questions.question: What language became the main medium of higher education?
questions.answer: English.

questions.question: What role did education play in spreading democratic ideas?
questions.answer: It introduced ideas of rights, equality, and representation.

questions.question: What was the purpose of colonial education policies?
questions.answer: To spread Western knowledge and support colonial administration.

questions.question: Which education policy supported English as the medium of instruction?
questions.answer: Macaulay’s Minute of 1835.

questions.question: What long-term institutions were created through colonial education?
questions.answer: Modern schools, colleges, and universities.

questions.question: What role did the educated middle class play in India?
questions.answer: They led social reforms and political movements.

questions.question: How did Western education influence Indian political thought?
questions.answer: It introduced democratic and nationalist ideas.

questions.question: What was one major impact of colonial education in India?
questions.answer: The development of modern education institutions.
